search.emptyShort: Type to search all pages and sections

common.contribBannerText: We’re looking for contributors — help build Nyra.
common.footerTagline: Fast · Safe · Minimal — systems language with LLVM under the hood.

index.tagline: Fast like a panther. Sharp by design.
index.lead: Go’s simplicity. Rust’s safety. C++’s speed — one toolchain, LLVM under the hood.
index.mvpBannerBody: Core and Stable Extended are semver-stable: async/await, ?, spawn, traits, serde, and enum payloads ship without W001. Prebuilt Linux, macOS, and Windows releases. A few advanced areas (multi-trait dyn, exotic generic serde) are still maturing — see Roadmap and status.md.
index.aboutDesc: Production-ready systems language — readable surface, strict core, LLVM-native speed.
index.aboutSourceP: Programs live in .ny files. A Nyra frontend emits LLVM IR, then clang links the Nyra runtime for I/O and runtime helpers.
index.aboutPhilP: Go-like control flow and a small keyword set, Rust-like compile-time memory rules, and native speed from LLVM optimizations.
index.aboutCliP: nyra run, build, test, fmt, and pkg — one toolchain from hello world to release binaries.
index.pillarsDesc: What we borrow — and what ships today.
index.pillarSafetyP: Ownership, auto-drop, NLL borrows — no GC, no leaks in normal code
index.pillarToolingP: run, build, test, fmt, pkg in one binary
index.pipeDetailFrontend: Lexer · Parser · Types · Ownership · Borrow · Lifetimes · Send/Sync
index.exploreDesc: Pick a path — beginner to reference app.
index.cardGettingP: Create a project, run Hello World, understand nyra run .
index.cardBasicsP: Variables, functions, loops, structs, enums, imports — with examples
index.cardBeginnerP: Eight step-by-step lessons — first program through a mini project
index.cardDungeonP: Full multi-file app — imports, match, tests
index.cardStdlibP: Strong stdlib — collections, FS, HTTP/TCP, crypto, DB, serde, WebSocket in-tree
index.cardMemoryP: Ownership, Drop plan, leak prevention, NLL, Send/Sync — with examples
index.cardLearningPathP: Zero to full app — step by step
index.aiSkillDesc: Download the canonical Nyra reference for Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLMs.
index.aiSkillBody: Single file nyra-skill.md — syntax, ownership, stdlib, PGO, escape analysis, stability tiers, and anti-hallucination guardrails (v0.0.1).
index.resultDesc: Stable Extended Result payloads with ? propagation for fallible pipelines.
index.resultTodayP: The ? operator, generic Result<T,E> via import "stdlib/option.ny", and tests such as tests/nyra/result_propagate_test.ny.
index.resultMissingP: Multi-trait dyn A + B objects and some exotic generic serde cases — see roadmap production gates.
index.resultProdP: Ready for production error-handling in CLI tools and servers on Core + Stable Extended.
index.statusBody: Nyra is production-ready on Core + Stable Extended (async, ?, spawn, traits, serde). Prebuilt releases for Linux, macOS, and Windows. See Roadmap, status.md, and stability policy.

getting-started.lead: Install Nyra, create a file, and run a program.
getting-started.p_1: You need clang on your PATH. See Installation for macOS, Linux, and Windows (including WSL).
getting-started.li_mod: nyra.mod — module manifest (module name; optional tls, require, link). Syntax: nyra.mod reference
getting-started.li_lock: nyra.lock / nyra.sum — dependency lock & checksums (empty until you add packages)
getting-started.li_main: main.ny — entry point with a Hello World sample
getting-started.p_files_detail: See NyraPkg → project files for what each file does, nyra.mod syntax, and how to add or remove a library by hand. HTTPS backends: TLS backends. nyra run . / nyra build . auto-sync require packages (fetch missing, prune removed — like Cargo).
getting-started.calloutBody: nyra run main.ny compiles a single file and does not load import dependencies. Multi-file apps must use nyra run . or nyra build .. See Modules.
getting-started.p_build: Binaries are written under target/debug/ or target/release/ (like Rust). Path defaults to . when omitted.
getting-started.next_0: Beginner track — Lesson 1 — step-by-step from your first program (recommended for new programmers)
getting-started.next_1: Built-in methods — every builtin with a usage example
getting-started.next_2: Learn Nyra — step-by-step tutorials with runnable code
getting-started.next_3: Imports guide — functions, structs, constants across files
getting-started.next_4: Language basics — variables, functions, loops, structs, imports
getting-started.next_7: Standard library — batteries-included design and implementation status
getting-started.next_9: Memory & ownership — NLL, lifetimes, Drop, Send/Sync

install.lead: Install the Nyra toolchain from a GitHub release on macOS, Linux, or Windows.
install.calloutTitle: Public repository required for the one-line installer
install.calloutBody: The repo must be public (or you must be logged in) to download release assets and to use the curl | sh installer. Private repos require manual download from the Releases page while signed in, or building from source.
install.td_clang_why: Links your .ny programs with the Nyra C runtime
install.li_tag_2: Choose a version (e.g. v0.0.1 — includes nyra pkg init + main.ny scaffold).
install.li_tag_3: Under Assets, download the file that matches your CPU and OS (see table).
install.p_checksum: Optional: verify checksums using SHA256SUMS on the same release page.
install.p_env: Set these environment variables (add to your shell profile so they persist):
install.h3_mac_b: Option B — Manual install from a release tag
install.h3_linux_b: Option B — Manual install from a release tag
install.p_win: Prebuilt Windows binaries are not published yet. Use one of these paths:
install.callout_wsl: Native Windows support is evolving; WSL2 matches the Linux release workflow today.
install.callout_multi_body: For apps with import, run from the project root: nyra run . — see Modules and Getting started.
install.p_src: If you work on the compiler itself:
install.p_dev: Local iteration: ./scripts/dev-install.sh from the repo root.

language.lead: Core syntax for Nyra v0.2.x — indentation-friendly blocks, minimal keywords. New to Nyra? Start with Language basics.

language-basics.lead: Short reference: variables, print, if, loops, and functions.

types.lead: Built-in types, user-defined structs and enums, arrays, and references.

imports.lead: Split your app into multiple `.ny` files and share functions, types, and constants with `import "path.ny"` — then build from the project root.

examples.lead: Copy-paste friendly mini apps that show how real Nyra projects are structured. Each example lists files, code, and the command to run.

dungeon-steps.lead: A small but **complete multi-file app** in the Nyra repository. Use it as a map from “I read the syntax page” to “I can structure a real project.”

tooling.lead: The nyra CLI — every command with a job and a copy-paste example: run, build, check, test, fmt, diag, watch, debug, lsp, dap, ide, and pkg.

languageVsEcosystem.lead: Nyra is a general-purpose language. Frameworks and packages complete it for each domain — the compiler does not bake in backend-only stacks.

packages.lead: Standalone package manager for Nyra — manifests, lockfiles, and dependencies. Repository: github.com/nyra-lang/pkg.

memory.lead: NLL borrow checking, lifetimes, HRTB, custom Drop, Send/Sync, and automatic heap drop.

stdlib.lead: Short stdlib/ reference — builtins on methods; modules below.

methods.lead: Quick lookup for builtins and method syntax — full examples in the gallery below.

reference.lead: Keywords, operators, literals, and quick lookup — everything the lexer and parser accept today.

keywords.lead: Every reserved word and type name the lexer treats as a keyword — plus how to reuse those names as variables with a leading @, and how compiler attributes differ (#[…], not @).

comptime.lead: Optional compile-time evaluation — lookup tables, string routing, and generated constants with zero runtime cost.

traits-macros.lead: Trait definitions, impl Drop for Type, and macro expansion.

performance.lead: LLVM opt, release flags, LTO, benchmarks.

pgo.lead: Ship a release binary tuned to how your program actually runs — one command, no manual LLVM steps. Nyra instruments, trains, merges profiles, and rebuilds for you.

escapeAnalysis.lead: Nyra proves which values stay local at compile time, then promotes them to the stack, inlines sequential channels without mutexes, and honors #[no_escape] on references.

ffi-abi.lead: Stable C ABI and heap ownership at the boundary.

editor-setup.lead: VS Code / Cursor: official extension, nyra lsp, and nyra dap debugging.

osHardware.lead: Read and write files, detect the operating system, query environment variables, and reach specialized hardware through the stdlib, FFI, or language bridge.

learning-path.lead: Structured path from zero to a full multi-file app.

beginner-track.lead: Step-by-step lessons for your first programs in Nyra — no prior experience required.

ai-skill.lead: Download nyra-skill.md — the canonical Nyra reference for AI assistants: syntax, types, functions, loops, structs, imports, ownership, stdlib, and anti-hallucination rules.

concurrency.lead: spawn with capture lowering, Send/Sync checks, and pthread-backed channels.

roadmap.lead: Nyra — Core and Stable Extended are semver-stable. See status.md and stability-v1.md.

learn-intro.lead: What Nyra is and how to read this track.

learn-get-started.lead: Install Nyra and run your first file.

learn-variables.lead: Bind a name to a value with let and let mut.

learn-while.lead: Repeat while the condition is true.

learn-data-structures.lead: Arrays, vectors, tuples, maps, structs, enums.
